items.title: STAT 992: Topics in Causal Inference
topics: average treatment effect on the treated (ATT)
influence functions and von Mises calculus
items.source_text: The course covers basic concepts in causal inference and randomized experiments, and identification under strong ignorability (i.e., no unmeasured confounding), including identification of the average treatment effect (ATE), the average treatment effect on the treated (ATT), and identification of static, single-time optimal treatment regime (OTR). Additional topics include identification under unmeasured confounding with instrumental variables.

items.title: ENVIR ST 413: Preserving Nature
items.source_text: The course aims to help students understand the theory and practice the skills of effective, scientific, ethical, and legitimate preservation of nature including biodiversity, the atmosphere, water, and more. Students learn from global lessons in how to intervene against threats to nature, and the roles of ethics, law, and research in preserving nature, including environmental provisions in national constitutions and U.S. federal-state-tribal governance as they relate to environmental protection.

items.title: CIV ENGR 571: Urban Transportation Planning
items.source_text: The course provides a broad overview of the elements of transportation planning, from policy and design to analysis and evaluation, covering planning frameworks, land use planning, modeling, and transportation demand management. The course examines the relationship between land use and mobility, with an emphasis on connecting land use decision-making with mobility planning, including smart growth and context-sensitive design practices.

items.title: POP HLTH 849: Genetic Epidemiology
items.source_text: The course provides an introduction to genetic epidemiology, particularly genetic association studies. Topics include a general overview of genetics and Mendelian and complex inheritance, as well as various elements of study design, including participant ascertainment; phenotype definition; biologic sample selection; genotyping, sequencing, and quality control; measurement of covariates, and choice of analytic methods.

items.title: PA 881: Cost-Benefit Analysis
topics: environmental and social policy case studies
items.source_text: The course presents the welfare economics underpinnings for evaluating the social benefits and costs of government activities. Issues such as uncertainty, the social discount rate, and welfare weights are discussed, with case studies from the environmental, social policy, and agricultural areas. Students work with real-world clients in the public, non-governmental, and private sectors to conduct a cost-benefit analysis and produce a report.

items.title: ED PSYCH 560: Foundations in Quantitative and Qualitative Research Methods
topics: theoretical foundations of social science inquiry
items.source_text: EdPsych 560 presents introductory research procedures in the social sciences, including the exploration of theoretical foundations and practical use of basic tools and programs needed for quantitative and qualitative data analysis. Students discover the theoretical foundations of regression analysis and apply it to real datasets using R software, and for the qualitative project, students perform observations and interviews and create qualitative coding schemes.
